The museum features exhibitions exploring painting, sculpture, film, and new media, with docent-led tours and free community events. Located in the heart of Silicon Valley, it serves as a cultural hub for the South Bay region.
attractions.hours: Museum + Store: Thursday 4–9pm, Friday 11am–9pm, Saturday–Sunday 11am–6pm. Museum Café: Thursday 4–9pm, Friday 11am–9pm, Saturday–Sunday 11am–3pm. An admission ticket is not required to visit the Museum Store or café.
attractions.admission: Adult: $20; Senior 65 and over: $15; Youth 17 and under: Free; College student + teacher with ID: Free. Museum members receive free unlimited entry.
highlights: Contemporary art exhibitions featuring painting, sculpture, film, and new media
Docent-led public tours offered Thursday at 5pm and Friday–Sunday at 1pm and 2:30pm
Free community events including First Fridays and Community Days
Museum Store with creative gifts and art-related merchandise
Museum Café serving gourmet sandwiches, soups, salads, and desserts
Located in downtown San José's vibrant cultural district
attractions.tips: Elevators are temporarily out of service — access to upper and lower levels is via stairs only. Admission tickets are not required to visit the Museum Store or café. Youth 17 and under, college students, and teachers with ID enter free. Check the specials page for discounted admission offers. Order tickets online in advance. Use the parking information on the website to plan your visit.
attractions.nearby: San Jose State University, San Pedro Square Market, Tech Interactive Museum, Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose, San Jose City Hall, History San Jose, San Jose Institute of Contemporary Art

attractions.name: Shoreline Amphitheatre
attractions.description: Shoreline Amphitheatre is a premier outdoor amphitheater located in Mountain View, California, in the San Francisco Bay Area. With a capacity of 22,500 (6,500 reserved seats and 16,000 general admission lawn), it is one of the largest music venues in the Bay Area. Built in 1985-1986 as part of the Shoreline Park project, it has hosted major music festivals like Lollapalooza and Ozzfest, as well as developer conferences such as Google I/O.
attractions.hours: Hours vary by event. The venue operates only on event days; no standard daily hours apply.
attractions.days_open: Event days only (varies by event schedule)
attractions.admission: Admission prices vary by event and seating tier. Prices depend on the specific concert or event being held.
highlights: 22,500-person capacity with 6,500 reserved seats and 16,000 lawn general admission
Hosted major music festivals including Lollapalooza, Ozzfest, and Rolling Loud
Home of annual Google I/O developer conference
Iconic Steal Your Face-inspired design by Bill Graham
Located within Shoreline Park with adjacent golf course
Premier Silicon Valley concert destination since 1986
attractions.tips: The venue is open only on event days, so check the schedule before visiting. Parking can be expensive and fills up quickly — consider carpooling or public transit. The lawn area is general admission, so arrive early for a good spot. Bring sunscreen and layers as the venue is outdoors. Food and beverage prices inside are high; check the venue policy on outside items.
attractions.nearby: Shoreline Park and Golf Course, NASA Ames Research Center, Computer History Museum, Google Campus, Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts, Caltrain Mountain View Station, Castro Street shopping and dining district

attractions.name: The Tech Interactive (San Jose Tech Museum of Innovation)
attractions.description: The Tech Interactive, formerly known as the San Jose Tech Museum of Innovation, is a hands-on science and technology center located in downtown San Jose. It features over 200 interactive exhibits that inspire visitors to explore innovation, design, and STEM concepts through experiential learning. The museum also houses an IMAX theater and the Bowers Institute for tech education programs.
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Friday: 10 a.m. – 3 p.m., Saturday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Sunday: 11 a.m. – 5 p.m., Monday: Closed. Note: Hours may vary; check the daily schedule for holidays and special closures.
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: General Admission: $38.00 (includes one Educational IMAX show on the day of visit). IMAX Hollywood movies priced separately. Membership available for year-round access. Prices include taxes & fees.
highlights: 200+ hands-on interactive STEM exhibits
IMAX Theater with educational and Hollywood films
Innovation Lab and design challenges
Bowers Institute for education and community programs
Live Tech Shows and demonstrations
Located in downtown San Jose near SAP Center and convention center
attractions.tips: General Admission includes an Educational IMAX show — select your film before checkout. Parking is available at nearby downtown garages (Convention Center Garage and 2nd & San Carlos Garage) with validation from The Tech. The museum is a 5-minute walk from VTA light rail Convention Center station. Visit on weekdays for smaller crowds; Saturdays tend to be busiest. Check the daily schedule on the website for show times and special events. Members receive free admission year-round.

attractions.name: Villa Montalvo (Montalvo Arts Center)
attractions.description: Villa Montalvo, now known as Montalvo Arts Center, is a historic arts and nature center nestled in the foothills above Saratoga in Silicon Valley. The 172-acre estate features over 100 acres of woodland hiking trails, formal gardens, an amphitheater, and a historic mansion. It serves as an oasis of arts and nature, hosting concerts, exhibitions, artist residencies, and community events year-round.
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM – Sunset, Daily
attractions.admission: Free admission to the grounds and gardens. Parking is free. Special events and concerts may require tickets.
highlights: Over 172 acres of woodland hiking trails and formal gardens
Historic Villa mansion built by Senator James D. Phelan in 1912
Outdoor amphitheater hosting concerts and performances
Contemporary art installations throughout the grounds
Artist residency programs supporting international multidisciplinary artists
Community events including festivals, family programs, and picnics
attractions.tips: Park hours are 8 AM to sunset daily. Grounds may be closed early or partially restricted for private events, so check the closures page before visiting. Prom photography is prohibited on weekends (Friday–Sunday). No picnic tables or barbecues are available, but visitors are welcome to bring blankets for picnics on the lawn. Free shuttle service is sometimes offered from West Valley College for special events.
attractions.nearby: Saratoga Village (downtown Saratoga), Hakone Estate and Gardens, Vasona Lake County Park, Sanborn County Park, Monte Bello Open Space Preserve, Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum of San Jose

attractions.name: Winchester Mystery House
attractions.description: The Winchester Mystery House is a Victorian mansion in San Jose, California, once the personal residence of Sarah Winchester, widow of firearms magnate William Wirt Winchester. Built over 36 years (1886-1922), the house is renowned for its size, architectural curiosities including staircases to nowhere and doors opening to nothing, and the numerous myths and legends surrounding its construction. The house sits on a 6-acre estate and features 160 rooms, only 110 of which are accessible on the standard tour.
attractions.hours: September - April: Weekdays 10 AM - 4 PM, Weekends 10 AM - 5 PM | May - August: Weekdays 10 AM - 5 PM, Weekends 10 AM - 7 PM. Estate fully closes 90 minutes after the final tour begins. Entrance gates close 15 minutes after the final tour departure.
attractions.days_open: Daily (open 364 days a year; closed Christmas Day)
attractions.admission: Mansion Tour: Adult $38.99, Senior $32.99, Child (5-12) $17.99, Children under 4 free | Basement Access Add-on: $13 (self-guided, ages 5+) | Basement & Attic Access Add-on: $19 (self-guided, ages 5+, May 1 - July 31) | Flashlight Tour: Adult $49.99, Child (5-12) $29.99 (special event) | Immersive Virtual 360° Tour: $8.99 | Video Access Tour: $5.99 rental (72 hrs) or $13.99 purchase | Bundle & Save: Add Explore More Tour for only $23 more
highlights: 65-minute guided Mansion Tour through 110 of 160 rooms
Architectural oddities including staircases to nowhere, doors opening to blank walls, and switchback stairs
Victorian gardens with a self-guided Garden Tour (included with Mansion Tour)
Sarah's Attic Shooting Gallery vintage arcade game
Café 13 serving breakfast and lunch daily
Limited-time Basement and Attic Access add-on exploring rarely seen areas
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance as tours sell out, especially on weekends and holidays. Children 4 and under are free but all ages are welcome on the Mansion Tour. The mansion is not wheelchair accessible due to its historic nature; an ADA tour is available as a separate option. Arrive at least 15 minutes before your tour time. Parking is free and onsite. Photography is allowed in the gardens but not during the Mansion Tour. Consider adding the Basement Access for an extra $13 for a more complete experience.
attractions.nearby: Santana Row (upscale shopping and dining), SAP Center (San Jose Sharks arena), San Jose Museum of Art, Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum, Winchester Mystery House is near Valley Fair Mall and within easy reach of Santa Cruz (coast) and the Santa Clara University campus.

attractions.name: Año Nuevo State Park
attractions.description: Año Nuevo State Park is a rugged peninsula in San Mateo County, California, renowned as one of the largest mainland breeding colonies in the world for northern elephant seals. The park encompasses Año Nuevo Island and Año Nuevo Point, featuring diverse plant communities including old growth forest, freshwater marsh, and knobcone pine forest. Visitors can observe elephant seals, sea lions, and sea otters year-round, with guided walks required during breeding season (December–March). The park is also a designated U.S. National Natural Landmark and IUCN Category III protected area.
attractions.hours: Day Use Area: 8:30 AM to Sunset; Seal Viewing Areas: 8:30 AM to 3:30 PM
attractions.admission: Regular Size Auto: $10.00; Senior (age 62+): $9.00; Bus (10-24 passengers): $50.00; Bus (25+ passengers): $100.00
highlights: One of the world's largest mainland breeding colonies for northern elephant seals
Year-round elephant seal viewing with self-guided access April–November and guided walks December–March
Diverse ecosystems including old growth forest, freshwater marsh, wetland, and knobcone pine forest
U.S. National Natural Landmark and IUCN Category III protected area
Marine Education Center with natural history exhibits and bookstore
Año Nuevo Island and coastal bluffs offering dramatic ocean views
attractions.tips: April–November: Self-guided viewing, no reservation needed. December–March: Guided walks are mandatory and must be booked in advance via ReserveCalifornia (reservations open 56 days ahead). The seal viewing hike is 3–4 miles round-trip over moderate, varied terrain including sand dunes. Wear layers and sturdy shoes — it can be cold, windy, and foggy. No pets, bikes, drones, umbrellas, or strollers allowed in the Nature Preserve. Allow 2.5–3 hours for the round-trip hike. Arrive early as parking can fill up on weekends. Equal Access Tours are available for visitors needing mobility assistance.
attractions.nearby: Pigeon Point Light Station State Historic Park, Butano State Park, Greyhound Rock State Marine Conservation Area, Año Nuevo State Marine Conservation Area, Pescadero State Beach, Bean Hollow State Beach, Big Basin Redwoods State Park

attractions.name: Burlingame Museum of PEZ Memorabilia
attractions.description: The Burlingame Museum of PEZ Memorabilia was a unique museum in Burlingame, California, dedicated entirely to PEZ dispensers and related collectibles. Opened in 1995 and run by Gary and Nancy Doss, it claimed to have an example of every PEZ dispenser ever sold, including over 1,000 different characters. The museum also featured a Classic Toy Museum and a Banned Toys Museum. It permanently closed in July 2019.
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Historical hours; museum is permanently closed as of July 2019)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Saturday (Closed Sunday and Monday; permanently closed July 2019)
attractions.admission: Adults (ages 10+): $5.00; Seniors (60+): $4.00; Children (4-12): $3.00; Children under 4: Free. First Thursday of the month: Free admission. (Historical pricing; museum is permanently closed)
highlights: World's largest PEZ dispenser on display
Example of every PEZ dispenser ever sold (1,000+ characters)
Wonder Woman dispenser autographed by Lynda Carter
Garfield dispenser autographed by Jim Davis
Classic Toy Museum featuring Lincoln Logs, Erector Sets, and more
Banned Toys Museum with recalled and banned toys
attractions.tips: The museum permanently closed in July 2019, so it is no longer open to visitors. Free street parking is available just south of the museum on California Drive. If you collect PEZ, the museum's website still offers PEZ products for sale online. First Thursday of each month offered free admission when the museum was open.
attractions.nearby: Burlingame Avenue (downtown shopping and dining), San Francisco International Airport (SFO), Coyote Point Recreation Area, Hillsdale Shopping Center, San Mateo County History Museum

attractions.name: Coyote Point Recreation Area
attractions.description: Coyote Point Recreation Area is a 670-acre regional park on San Francisco Bay in San Mateo, offering picnicking, swimming, windsurfing, biking, jogging, fishing, boating, sailing, playgrounds, and CuriOdyssey science museum with native wildlife exhibits. It features a sandy beach, marina, trails along the Bay Trail, and views of planes landing at SFO and windsurfers. Families enjoy the castle-themed Magic Mountain Playground and wildlife viewing in marshes and aviaries.
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM (seasonal, shorter in winter)
attractions.days_open: Daily (gates sunrise to sunset; confirm for holidays)
attractions.admission: $6 per vehicle entry fee (waivers for seniors Mon-Fri non-holidays, veterans, disabled, etc.; free for walk-ins)
highlights: Magic Mountain Playground with castle and dragons
CuriOdyssey museum and native California wildlife zoo
Sandy beach for swimming and windsurfing/kiteboarding
Marina, yacht club, and boating facilities
Hiking and biking trails with Bay Trail sections
Views of SFO planes, bay bridges, and birdwatching
Picnic areas and rifle range
attractions.tips: Arrive early for parking on weekends; bring cash or card for $6 fee (machines available); no pets allowed inside park (ok on leashed on Bay Trail); windy so layer clothing; free entry for walkers/bikers; check for playground closures; great for plane spotting and picnics.
attractions.nearby: CuriOdyssey (inside park), Poplar Creek Golf Course (adjacent), San Mateo Japanese Garden (1.7 mi), Sawyer Camp Trail (1.5 mi), Seal Point Park (1.7 mi), San Francisco International Airport (overhead flights).

attractions.name: Filoli Historic House & Garden
attractions.description: Filoli is a country estate featuring a Georgian Revival mansion built in 1917, 16 acres of formal English Renaissance gardens, and over 650 acres of natural lands with hiking trails and diverse ecosystems. Located on the Peninsula near Woodside, it preserves the legacy of the Bourn and Roth families and is now managed by a nonprofit open to the public. Visitors can explore the house museum, stunning seasonal blooms, and nature preserve.Filoli.org, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: 10am–5pm (House 11am–4pm)
attractions.admission: Adults ~$35-45, Seniors/Children 5-17 discounted (~$39 adult, $47 senior, $39 child via resellers), under 5 free; advance timed tickets required, book ahead for weekends. Members free.[Filoli.org](https://filoli.org/visit/), [Fever](https://feverup.com/m/135294), [Kessler Elsewhere](https://www.kesslerelsewhere.com/blog/a-guide-to-filoli-historic-house-and-garden)
highlights: 16 acres of English Renaissance formal gardens with seasonal blooms
Georgian Revival historic house museum
Over 650 acres of natural lands with hiking trails (California Trail, Spring Creek Trail)
Sunken Garden and yew-lined avenues
6.8-acre orchard and working garden
Year-round events like Summer Nights and Holidays at Filoli
attractions.tips: Book timed tickets in advance, especially weekends. Wear comfortable shoes for walking; visit in spring for peak blooms or summer for roses. Allow 2-4 hours; explore house, gardens, and trails. Members get priority access.[Filoli.org](https://filoli.org/visit/visitor-guide/), [SF Travel](https://www.sftravel.com/article/six-things-to-do-filoli)
attractions.nearby: Huddart Park, Wunderlich County Park, Windy Hill Open Space Preserve, Purisima Creek Redwoods (hiking); Woodside town center.[Tripadvisor](https://www.tripadvisor.com/AttractionsNear-g33292-d113585-Filoli_Historic_House_Gardens-Woodside_California.html), [SF Peninsula](https://www.thesanfranciscopeninsula.com/blog/stories/post/a-day-in-woodside-california/)

attractions.name: Half Moon Bay State Beach
attractions.description: Half Moon Bay State Beach is a 4-mile stretch of protected sandy beaches in California's state park system, comprising Francis, Venice, Dunes, and Roosevelt Beaches. The 181-acre park, established in 1956, offers day-use areas for sunbathing, picnicking, fishing, and surfing, along with a campground at Francis Beach.California State Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Sunrise to sunset; Visitor Center Sat-Sun (weather permitting)
attractions.admission: Vehicle day use/parking: $10; accepts annual passes; seniors $9, disabled $5[California State Parks fee list](https://www.parks.ca.gov/pages/737/files/Current_web_day%20use.pdf), [ThatsMyPark](https://thatsmypark.org/parks-and-beaches/half-moon-bay-state-beach/)
highlights: 4 miles of sandy beaches (Francis, Venice, Dunes, Roosevelt)
Campground with 52 sites for tents/RVs at Francis Beach
Coastside Trail for hiking, biking, jogging
Surfing, fishing, picnicking, sunbathing
Visitor Center with exhibits on ecology and history
Rich wildlife including snowy plovers, seals, whales
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends as parking lots fill quickly. No beach fires or drones allowed; dogs on leash max 6ft, not on beaches. Check for Roosevelt Beach construction closures. Bring cash for some kiosks; annual pass recommended for frequent visits.[California State Parks](https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=531), [Half Moon Bay city](https://www.halfmoonbay.gov/732/Beaches)
attractions.nearby: Pillar Point Harbor, Poplar Beach, Mavericks surf spot, Pescadero Marsh State Natural Preserve, Bean Hollow State Beach[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Half_Moon_Bay_State_Beach), [California State Parks](https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=531)

attractions.name: Iris & B. Gerald Cantor Center for Visual Arts at Stanford University
attractions.description: The Cantor Arts Center is an art museum on the campus of Stanford University in Stanford, California, featuring over 38,000 works spanning 5,000 years of art history across 24 galleries. It houses the largest collection of Auguste Rodin sculptures outside of Paris, with 199 works. The museum is free and open to the public, with no admission charge required.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 a.m.–6:00 p.m. | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 11:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m. | Friday: 11:00 a.m.–6:00 p.m. | Saturday: 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. | Sunday: 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m.
attractions.admission: Free admission for all visitors
highlights: Largest collection of Auguste Rodin sculptures outside Paris with 199 works
Over 38,000 items spanning 5,000 years of art history across 24 galleries
B. Gerald Cantor Rodin Sculpture Garden with outdoor bronze sculptures
Collections including African Art, American Art, Ancient Art, Asian Art, and the Andy Warhol Photography Archive
Adjacent Anderson Collection featuring 121 works of post-WWII American art
Asian American Art Initiative (AAAI) established in 2021
attractions.tips: Parking is limited and managed through ParkMobile (contactless payment). Visitor parking is free on weekends. Free parking on weekends as noted on the museum's website. Groups of 10 or more should register in advance. Strollers are available for visitors. The museum is wheelchair accessible with an accessible drop-off on Lomita Drive. Photography is allowed for personal and non-commercial use. Check with visitor services for the most up-to-date information on tours and events.
attractions.nearby: Anderson Collection at Stanford University, Stanford University campus, Stanford Memorial Church, Dish (Stanford hiking trail), Stanford Shopping Center

attractions.name: James V. Fitzgerald Marine Reserve
attractions.description: The James V. Fitzgerald Marine Reserve is a 32-acre marine protected area in Moss Beach, California, featuring exceptional tidepool habitats, coastal bluffs, marshes, and cypress forests. It protects a diverse intertidal community including sea stars, anemones, crabs, and rare species, and is part of the Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary. Established in 1969 and named after a local conservationist, it offers educational opportunities and is popular for tidepooling at low tide.San Mateo County Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Opens daily at 8:00 AM; closing times vary seasonally: Dec-Feb 5 PM, Mar 6-7 PM, Apr-Aug 7 PM, Sep 7-8 PM, Oct 6-7 PM, Nov 5-6 PM (check site for exact dates and changes)
attractions.admission: Free admission and parking
highlights: World-class tide pools with diverse marine life like sea stars, anemones, crabs, urchins
Blufftop trails through century-old cypress groves
Harbor seals visible at low tide
Part of Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary
Educational naturalist programs on weekends/holidays
Rare species and high biodiversity (over 1000 species estimated)
attractions.tips: Visit at low tide (check tide charts for tides below 1 ft, ideally negative) for best tidepooling; explore 1-2 hours around low tide. Never turn your back on the ocean; no touching animals/plants. No dogs allowed. Groups of 15+ need reservations. Bring binoculars for seals; stay distant from pups (Mar-Jun).
attractions.nearby: Pillar Point Harbor, Half Moon Bay, Moss Beach Distillery, Mavericks surf spot, Montara State Beach

attractions.name: Pigeon Point Light Station State Historic Park
attractions.description: Perched on a cliff 50 miles south of San Francisco, the 115-foot Pigeon Point Lighthouse is one of the tallest lighthouses on the West Coast and has been guiding mariners since 1872. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places and designated a California Historical Landmark, the lighthouse currently remains closed to the public for restoration but can be viewed from the grounds. The park features original historic buildings including the fog signal building (now housing the original Fresnel lens), former lighthouse keepers' homes converted to a hostel, and scenic coastal trails.
attractions.hours: Day-use Area: 8:00 AM to Sunset, daily. Visitor Center: Friday–Monday, 10:00 AM–4:00 PM. Park Store: Friday–Monday, 10:00 AM–4:00 PM. Guided history walks: Sundays at 2:00 PM.
attractions.days_open: Daily (grounds open every day; visitor center and store open Friday through Monday)
attractions.admission: Admission and parking are free. No tickets required.
highlights: 115-foot tall lighthouse, one of the tallest on the West Coast
Original Fresnel lens on display in the fog signal building
Historic lighthouse keepers' homes converted to a hostel
Scenic Pacific Ocean cliff-top views and coastal trails
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places
Guided history walks and Junior Ranger programs
attractions.tips: The lighthouse tower itself is closed for restoration and cannot be climbed, but visitors can still enjoy the grounds, historic buildings, and ocean views. Visit at sunset for spectacular coastal lighting. Bring layers — the coast can be windy and foggy even on warm days. Dogs are not permitted on the grounds (except service animals). Rocky Point area is closed to protect nesting birds. The park store is only open Friday–Monday, so plan accordingly if you want to purchase souvenirs.
attractions.nearby: Pescadero State Beach, Butano State Park, Año Nuevo State Park, Half Moon Bay, San Gregorio State Beach

attractions.name: San Mateo Japanese Garden
attractions.description: The San Mateo Japanese Garden, located within San Mateo Central Park, is one of the finest Japanese gardens in California. Designed by landscape architect Nagao Sakurai of the Imperial Palace of Tokyo, it features a granite pagoda, tea house, koi pond, bamboo grove, and traditional Japanese landscaping. The garden is a tranquil oasis among the high-rise buildings of downtown San Mateo and reflects the sister-city relationship between San Mateo and Toyonaka, Japan.
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday–Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Five-level stone pagoda donated by Toyonaka, Japan
Koi pond with fish feedings at 11 AM and 3 PM (spring/summer)
Traditional tea house
Cherry blossoms in late winter/early spring
Bamboo grove and Japanese maple trees
Drum Bridge (crescent-shaped bridge)
attractions.tips: Visit in late winter or early spring for spectacular cherry blossoms. Arrive at 11 AM or 3 PM for koi fish feeding (spring/summer only). No dogs are allowed in the Japanese Garden. Parking is available in the structure beneath the adjacent tennis courts. The garden is small—plan for a 15-30 minute visit. No food or drinks are allowed inside the garden.
attractions.nearby: Central Park, San Mateo Arboretum Society, Sawyer Camp Trail, Coyote Point Recreation Area, CuriOdyssey, Hiller Aviation Museum, Hillsdale Shopping Center, Seal Point Park

attractions.name: Stanford University Campus
attractions.description: Stanford University, officially Leland Stanford Junior University, is a private research university founded in 1885 by railroad magnate Leland Stanford and his wife Jane in memory of their son. Spanning 8,180 acres, it is one of the largest university campuses in the United States, featuring Spanish Colonial Revival architecture, world-class museums, research facilities, and the famous Hoover Institution. Located in the heart of Silicon Valley, Stanford has been a major catalyst for the development of the tech industry.
attractions.hours: The campus is open to visitors 24/7. Specific buildings, museums, and facilities have their own operating hours.
attractions.admission: Free to visit the campus grounds. Some museums and facilities may charge separate admission fees.
highlights: Main Quad with its distinctive Spanish Colonial Revival architecture
Rodin Sculpture Garden featuring works by Auguste Rodin
Hoover Institution on War, Revolution, and Peace
Cantor Arts Center with an extensive art collection
Stanford Memorial Church
8,180-acre campus — one of the largest in the nation
attractions.tips: Campus is open to the public 24/7 but parking can be limited; consider using public parking lots or riding a bike. Visit the Visitor Center at 295 Galvez Street for maps and guided tour information. The Cantor Arts Center and Rodin Sculpture Garden are free and open to the public. Weekdays are busier with student activity; weekends offer a quieter experience. Check individual building hours before visiting specific museums or facilities.
attractions.nearby: Stanford Shopping Center, Cantor Arts Center, Rodin Sculpture Garden, Dish (Stanford hiking area), Sand Hill Road, Palo Alto Downtown, Computer History Museum (Mountain View), Googleplex (Mountain View)

attractions.name: Big Basin Redwoods State Park
attractions.description: Established in 1902, Big Basin Redwoods State Park is California's oldest state park, located in the Santa Cruz Mountains. It preserves the largest continuous stand of ancient coast redwoods south of San Francisco, with trees over 1,000 years old, diverse habitats, and over 80 miles of trails. Severely impacted by the 2020 CZU Lightning Complex Fire which burned 97% of the park, it has partially reopened for limited day-use with ongoing recovery and rebuilding efforts.California State Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: 8 a.m. to sunset
attractions.admission: $6 vehicle day-use fee + $2 reservation fee ($10 without reservation); free for foot/bike/bus arrivals. Reservations encouraged due to limited parking.[California State Parks](https://www.parks.ca.gov/bigbasin), [Reimagining Big Basin](https://reimaginingbigbasin.org/visit/)
highlights: California's oldest state park (1902)
Ancient coast redwoods up to 1,800 years old
Over 80 miles of trails including Redwood Loop and Skyline-to-the-Sea
Rancho del Oso coastal unit with beach access
Diverse wildlife and ecosystems
Ongoing recovery from 2020 fire with resilient redwood regrowth
attractions.tips: Reserve parking in advance online or by phone at (831) 338-8867. Bring water as potable water may not be available. Check current trail conditions and closures on the official website. Arrive by foot, bike, or bus to avoid fees and parking limits. Dogs allowed in limited areas on leash; no drones.[California State Parks](https://www.parks.ca.gov/bigbasin), [ThatsMyPark](https://thatsmypark.org/parks-and-beaches/big-basin-redwoods-state-park/)
attractions.nearby: Castle Rock State Park (connected by Skyline-to-the-Sea Trail), Rancho del Oso/Waddell Beach, Año Nuevo State Park, Butano State Park.[Reimagining Big Basin](https://reimaginingbigbasin.org/visit/), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Big_Basin_Redwoods_State_Park)

attractions.name: Henry Cowell Redwoods State Park
attractions.description: Henry Cowell Redwoods State Park is a California state park preserving mainly forest and riparian areas in the watershed of the San Lorenzo River, including a grove of old-growth coast redwood. Located in Santa Cruz County, primarily between the cities of Santa Cruz and Scotts Valley, near the community of Felton and UC Santa Cruz. The 4,623-acre park was established in 1953 and features the famous Redwood Grove Trail through ancient redwoods.
attractions.address: Redwood Grove Loop Trail, Felton, CA 95018
attractions.hours: Sunset to sunrise (day use); campground open year-round with reservations
attractions.admission: Day Use: $10 per vehicle (California State Parks fee); Senior (62+): $5; Dog: $2 additional per dog. Camping: $35-$45 per night depending on site type. Roaring Camp & Big Trees Railroad access available separately.
highlights: Redwood Grove Loop Trail through old-growth coast redwoods
4,623 acres of redwood forest, riparian areas, and chaparral
Roaring Camp & Big Trees Railroad steam train rides
Extensive hiking and mountain biking trail network
Camping facilities including family campsites and group sites
Fall Creek Unit with additional 2,390 acres of wilderness
attractions.tips: Visit early morning or weekdays to avoid crowds on the popular Redwood Grove Loop Trail. The main visitor center and parking lot are near Felton. Wear layers as temperatures vary between the forest canopy and open areas. Bring water as there are limited refill stations on trails. The park connects to the Santa Cruz Mountains trail system for longer hikes. Reservations are strongly recommended for camping, especially on weekends.
attractions.nearby: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk, UC Santa Cruz Campus, Natural Bridges State Beach, Big Basin Redwoods State Park, Roaring Camp Railroads, Felton Covered Bridge County Park, Wilder Ranch State Park

attractions.name: Monterey Bay Aquarium
attractions.description: The Monterey Bay Aquarium is a nonprofit public aquarium in Monterey, California, known for its regional focus on the marine habitats of Monterey Bay. It was the first to exhibit a living kelp forest when it opened in October 1984, and its biologists pioneered the animal husbandry of jellyfish and were the first to successfully care for and display a great white shark. The aquarium receives around two million visitors each year and is recognized for its ocean conservation efforts, research programs, and educational exhibits.
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m. PT; Saturday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m. PT; Sunday: 10 a.m.–6 p.m. PT. Members get early entry at 9:30 a.m. on weekends. Open every day except December 25.
attractions.admission: Adult (ages 18–69): $65; Youth (ages 5–17): $50; Senior (ages 70+): $50; Four and under: Free. Members enter free. Free admission for up to four people available at the Main Entrance for valid SNAP EBT/CalFresh cardholders with matching photo ID (Museums for All program).
highlights: Living Kelp Forest exhibit with daily diver feedings
Playful southern sea otters with training and feeding shows
Into the Deep — groundbreaking deep-sea exhibition
Open Sea wing with sardine shoals, tuna, and shark species
Seafood Watch program — influential sustainable seafood advisory
Jelly displays including the mesmerizing jelly gallery
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets in advance online as they are non-refundable and often sell out. Arrive early (members can enter at 9:30 a.m.) to avoid crowds. Plan 3–4 hours for your visit. Don't miss the sea otter feedings and Kelp Forest feeding shows. Wear layers — the aquarium can be cool inside. Parking is limited and expensive; consider public transit or rideshare. The Monterey area offers miles of beaches and hiking trails nearby.
attractions.nearby: Cannery Row (historic waterfront district), Monterey State Historic Park, Fisherman's Wharf Monterey, Monterey Bay Coastal Recreation Trail, Old Monterey City Hall, Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary, 17-Mile Drive, Point Lobos State Natural Reserve, Carmel-by-the-Sea

attractions.name: Natural Bridges State Beach
attractions.description: Natural Bridges State Beach is a 65-acre popular state park on the western edge of Santa Cruz, known for its iconic wave-carved sea arch, family-friendly beach, tide pools, and monarch butterfly migration. The park features a Monarch Grove Natural Preserve that hosts over 100,000 monarch butterflies each winter, accessible boardwalks, hiking trails, and excellent tidepooling opportunities within the Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary.
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM to Sunset
attractions.admission: Day Use Vehicle Fee: $10 (Senior $9, age 62+); Bus Parking (10-24 passengers): $50; Bus Parking (25+ passengers): $100. No camping available.
highlights: Iconic wave-carved natural sea arch, the last remaining of three original arches
Monarch Grove Natural Preserve hosting over 100,000 monarch butterflies each winter (October-February)
Rich tidepools within the Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary featuring sea anemones, sea stars, and hermit crabs
Accessible interpretive boardwalk through the monarch grove (0.13 mile)
Scenic beach with views of whales, seals, sea otters, and seabirds
Visitor center with interactive displays, videos, and bookstore
attractions.tips: Visit during low tide for the best tidepooling experience—check tide tables before your trip. Plan a visit between October and February to witness the monarch butterfly migration. No lifeguards are on duty, so exercise caution in the water. Dogs are allowed only in parking lots and picnic areas, not on the beach or trails. Beach wheelchairs are available free of charge with advance reservation. Arrive early on weekends and holidays as parking can fill up quickly. The visitor center is open Monday-Friday 11 AM to 4 PM.
attractions.nearby: Wilder Ranch State Park, Henry Cowell Redwoods State Park, New Brighton State Beach, Seacliff State Beach, Lighthouse Field State Beach, Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk, Santa Cruz Wharf

attractions.name: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk
attractions.description: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk is California's largest admission-free seaside amusement park, operating since 1907. It features over 40 rides and attractions along a beautiful sandy beach, including the historic Giant Dipper wooden roller coaster. The Boardwalk is a beloved family destination combining classic carnival fun with beach activities.
attractions.hours: Varies by day and season. In April 2026: Rides typically open 11AM-7PM or 12PM-7PM depending on the day (limited rides on some weekdays); Arcades typically open 10AM-11PM or 11AM-10PM. Weekend hours are extended (11AM-8PM on Saturdays). Hours change seasonally — check the official hours calendar before visiting.
attractions.days_open: Open most days of the week; rides are closed on some weekdays (e.g., Fridays and select other days in April 2026). Arcades are generally open daily.
attractions.admission: Park admission is FREE. Ride Wristbands: starting at $44.95 + tax (unlimited rides all day, price varies by date). Season Passes: starting at $104.95 + tax (valid through 12/31/26). MyBoardwalk Cards: $50, $100, or $200 (stored value for rides, arcades, games, food, and retail). Buy online to save up to 20% on wristbands.
highlights: Giant Dipper — historic wooden roller coaster (National Historic Landmark, operating since 1924)
Looff Carousel — beautifully restored 1911 carousel
Over 40 rides and attractions including water rides and thrill coasters
Free beach access with sandy shoreline
Casino Arcade and Neptune's Kingdom family entertainment center
Classic boardwalk food including famous corn dogs and saltwater taffy
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds, especially on weekends. Buy ride wristbands online in advance to save up to 20%. The park is admission-free — you only pay for rides and activities. Parking can be limited during peak season; consider using public transit or rideshare. Check the hours calendar as ride hours vary significantly by day. The Beach is free and open daily.
attractions.nearby: Santa Cruz Wharf, Natural Bridges State Beach, UC Santa Cruz Campus, Santa Cruz Surfing Museum, Henry Cowell Redwoods State Park, Monterey Bay Aquarium (30 minutes south)

attractions.name: Seymour Marine Discovery Center
attractions.description: The Seymour Marine Discovery Center is a community-supported marine science education center of the University of California, Santa Cruz, located on the bluff overlooking Monterey Bay. Visitors can touch sharks, hold hermit crabs, explore hands-on interactive touch pools, and learn about UCSC's ocean research. The center features live Monterey Bay marine life and is designed to be family-friendly with rotating discovery stations for all ages.
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Monday: Closed
attractions.admission: Adult: $12.00; Child (ages 3-17): $9.00; Children under 3: Free; Senior (65+): $11.00; Student (with ID): $9.00; UCSC students: Free; Members: Free. Tickets are non-refundable and valid for 30 days.
highlights: Hands-on interactive touch pools with live sharks, jellies, and hermit crabs
Panoramic bluff-top views overlooking Monterey Bay
Daily 30-minute guided tours led by knowledgeable volunteer docents
Rotating discovery stations designed for all ages, including kid-friendly scavenger hunts
Live Monterey Bay marine life exhibits showcasing local ocean biodiversity
UCSC Coastal Campus location with stroller-friendly and ADA accessible grounds
attractions.tips: Bring sunscreen and a jacket — the bluff location can be windy and sunny. Free parking on weekends; paid ParkMobile parking on weekdays. Arrive early to secure a spot on the popular 30-minute tours as they fill up quickly. Most visits take 1-2 hours. Bring lunch to enjoy on the outdoor benches with ocean views. The 1-mile loop trail around the UCSC Coastal Campus is a nice addition if you have extra time.
attractions.nearby: UC Santa Cruz Campus, Natural Bridges State Beach, Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk, West Cliff Drive, Santa Cruz Wharf, New Brighton State Beach, Long Marine Lab

attractions.name: Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve
attractions.description: Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve preserves 805 acres of majestic old-growth coast redwoods (Sequoia sempervirens) in Sonoma County. The reserve features some of the tallest and oldest redwood trees on Earth, including the 310-foot-tall Parson Jones Tree and the 1,400-year-old Colonel Armstrong Tree. Originally set aside by lumberman Colonel James Armstrong in the 1870s, the grove became a State Natural Reserve in 1964.
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM to one hour after official sunset (daily); Visitor Center: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM most days
attractions.admission: $10.00 per vehicle; $9.00 per vehicle for seniors (62+); $5.00 per vehicle with State Parks disabled access pass; $50.00 per bus (10-24 passengers); Free for pedestrians and bicyclists
highlights: The Parson Jones Tree - tallest tree in the grove at over 310 feet
The Colonel Armstrong Tree - oldest tree, estimated over 1,400 years old
The Icicle Tree - distinctive burl formations
Pioneer Nature Trail - 1.5 mile ADA accessible self-guided trail
Discovery Trail with Braille interpretive panels and tree hugging platform
9.2 miles of hiking trails through old-growth redwood forest
attractions.tips: Park in the main lot at the entrance and walk in for free rather than paying the vehicle fee to drive into the park. Dogs are not allowed on any trails - only on paved roads. Layered clothing is recommended due to variable weather with morning fog. Arrive early on weekends as parking fills up. Cell phone coverage is spotty in the park. The best way to experience the redwoods is walking, not driving.
attractions.nearby: Austin Creek State Recreation Area (3 miles above, with Bullfrog Pond Campground), Russian River (Guerneville), Korbel Champagne Cellars, Downtown Guerneville, Sonoma County wineries

attractions.name: Castello di Amorosa
attractions.description: Castello di Amorosa is an authentically styled 14th-century Tuscan castle winery located in Napa Valley's Calistoga. The castle features 107 rooms across multiple levels including a Chapel, Great Hall, Knights Hall, and extensive wine cellars. Built by Italian-American businessman Dario Sattui, it offers guided tours, wine tastings, and luxury experiences in a stunning medieval-inspired setting. Reservations are required for all visitors.
attractions.hours: Weekdays: 10:00am – 5:30pm; Weekends: 10:00am – 6:00pm; Daily (by reservation): 10:00am – 5:30pm. Holiday exceptions: Thanksgiving Day CLOSED, Christmas Eve 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM, Christmas Day CLOSED.
attractions.admission: Tasting Experience (seated reserve tasting, 5 selections): $60 (Club: Complimentary for 1 member & 1 guest). Diamond Estate Tour & Wine Tasting (guided, 5 selections): $75 (Club: $37.50). Cheese & Charcuterie Wine Pairing Tour (6 wines): $115 (Club: $92). Cabernet Is King Experience: $175–$195. Luxury Private Tours: Pricing upon request. Upgrades: Flagship Cab Tasting +$25, Chocolate Upgrade +$7.50, King's Ransom Chocolate Pairing +$28, Cheese & Charcuterie Box +$38.
highlights: Authentic 14th-century Tuscan-style castle with 107 rooms including Chapel, Great Hall, and Knights Hall
Guided Diamond Estate Tour exploring upper levels of the castle with wine educator
Award-winning Reserve and Current-Release wines including highly acclaimed Cabernet Sauvignons
Cheese & Charcuterie Wine Pairing Tour featuring 6 wines with local artisan pairings
Cabernet Is King immersive experience with sparkling wine welcome and food accompaniments
Stunning hillside location with rolling vineyard views in Napa Valley
attractions.tips: Reservations are required for everyone in your party — book in advance. Visit before noon for the best experience per TripAdvisor. Wear comfortable walking shoes; high heels are not recommended. No strollers, selfie sticks, or drones allowed. Picnicking is not permitted on the property. Tastings are limited to 75 minutes. All online bookings include a $4.95 booking fee. Full refunds available up to 24 hours before reservation.
attractions.nearby: Calistoga Hot Springs, Old Faithful Geyser of California, Sterling Vineyards, Schramsberg Vineyards, Petrified Forest, Mount Saint Helena, Napa Valley Museum, Chateau Montelena, Castello di Amorosa is about 5 miles north of St. Helena and 3 miles south of downtown Calistoga on Highway 29.

attractions.name: Charles M. Schulz Museum and Research Center
attractions.description: The Charles M. Schulz Museum and Research Center is a museum dedicated to the works of Charles M. Schulz, creator of the Peanuts comic strip. The museum opened on August 17, 2002, and is located in Santa Rosa, California. It features original Peanuts strips, artwork by Schulz, three rotating galleries, and a re-creation of Schulz's art studio.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 am–5:00 pm; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 11:00 am–5:00 pm; Thursday: 11:00 am–5:00 pm; Friday: 11:00 am–5:00 pm; Saturday: 10:00 am–5:00 pm; Sunday: 10:00 am–5:00 pm
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesdays year-round; also closed New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Fourth of July, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve Day, and Christmas Day)
attractions.admission: Members — FREE; Adults — $15; Seniors (62 and over) — $10; Students (with valid student ID) — $7; Youth (4-18) — $7; Children (3 and under) — FREE; EBT Cardholders (with card; up to 4 people within household) — $4; Military — $1 off admission
highlights: Original Peanuts comic strips and artwork by Charles M. Schulz
Re-creation of Charles M. Schulz's art studio
Three rotating galleries with exhibits that change every six months
3.5-ton wood sculpture of Snoopy by Yoshiteru Otani
22-foot ceramic mural made of 3,588 Peanuts strips depicting Lucy van Pelt holding the football for Charlie Brown
Animated Peanuts specials in the museum theater
attractions.tips: The museum is closed on Tuesdays year-round, so plan accordingly. Tickets are purchased on arrival (pre-sale not available). Arrive early on weekends as the museum opens at 10:00 am on Saturday and Sunday. EBT cardholders can get significant discounts for their household. The museum is also closed on major holidays including New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Fourth of July, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day.
attractions.nearby: Snoopy's Home Ice (Redwood Empire Ice Arena), Charles M. Schulz-Sonoma County Airport, Luther Burbank Home & Gardens, Santa Rosa Plaza, Sonoma County Museum, Jack London State Historic Park, Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve

attractions.name: Cornerstone Sonoma
attractions.description: Cornerstone Sonoma is a dynamic marketplace and art-inspired garden destination located at the gateway of Sonoma and Napa County. It features boutique wineries and tasting rooms, artisanal food vendors, locally-sourced restaurants, art installations, and beautifully landscaped gardens. The property also offers versatile indoor-outdoor event venues for weddings, corporate events, and community gatherings.
attractions.hours: Gardens: Daily 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Marketplace/Tasting Rooms: Daily 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (hours may vary by vendor; some vendors operate on limited days). Note: The property is currently in transition with some vendors closed — check ahead before visiting.
attractions.days_open: Daily (Wednesday through Sunday for most vendors; gardens typically open daily)
attractions.admission: Free admission to grounds and gardens. Individual vendors (tasting rooms, shops, restaurants) set their own prices.
highlights: Art-inspired botanical gardens with walking paths and pond views
Boutique wine tasting rooms including Meadowcroft Wines
Artisanal food vendors and locally-sourced restaurants
Outdoor fire pits and lounge areas with vineyard views
Event venues for weddings and corporate gatherings
Local artisan shops and marketplace vendors
attractions.tips: Check ahead before visiting as the property is in transition with some vendors closed. The gardens are free to explore and offer beautiful photo opportunities. Garden tours may be booked through the website. Weekend hours may be more limited — weekdays typically offer the fullest experience. Bring a camera for the scenic vineyard and garden backdrops.
attractions.nearby: Sonoma Plaza, Buena Vista Winery, Sebastiani Vineyards, Sonoma Mission Inn & Spa, Jack London State Historic Park, Chateau St. Jean

attractions.name: Napa Valley
attractions.description: Napa Valley is one of the world's premier wine regions, renowned for its Cabernet Sauvignon and over 400 wineries spread across 16 distinct appellations. Located in Northern California, the valley spans approximately 30 miles and features five charming towns, world-class dining including 15 Michelin-rated restaurants, hot air balloon rides, hiking trails, and luxury spas. The region produces only 4% of California's wine but is globally celebrated for quality, with 95% of its wineries being family-owned.
attractions.hours: The Napa Valley region itself is always open; individual wineries, restaurants, and attractions have varying hours. The Visit Napa Valley Welcome Center is open daily. Wineries typically operate 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM or later, with many open 7 days a week.
attractions.days_open: Daily (applicable to the region and most attractions)
attractions.admission: Free to visit the region. Winery tasting fees vary from $25–$100+ per person depending on the winery and experience level. Welcome Center visit is free.
highlights: Over 400 wineries across 16 appellations, with 95% family-owned
15 Michelin-rated restaurants offering world-class farm-to-table dining
Hot air balloon rides providing aerial views of vineyards
Five distinct towns: Calistoga, St. Helena, Yountville, Napa, and American Canyon
Luxury spas, wellness retreats, and boutique accommodations
Scenic hiking trails through state parks and redwood-covered creeks
attractions.tips: Make winery reservations in advance, especially during harvest season (August–October). Consider hiring a driver or booking a wine tour to avoid driving after tastings. Visit during spring or fall for fewer crowds and pleasant weather. The Welcome Center at 1300 1st St in Napa offers free maps and personalized trip planning. Many wineries require appointments, so plan your itinerary ahead of time.
attractions.nearby: Sonoma Valley Wine Country, Napa Valley Wine Train, Castello di Amorosa, Calistoga Hot Springs, Robert Mondavi Winery, Beringer Vineyards, V. Sattui Winery, Napa Valley Museum, Carneros Wine Region, Lake Berryessa

attractions.name: Napa Valley Wine Train
attractions.description: The Napa Valley Wine Train is a privately operated excursion train that runs between Napa and St. Helena, California, offering gourmet dining experiences aboard vintage railcars. Established in 1989, the train travels through the heart of Napa Valley's wine country, passing vineyards and small towns including Yountville, Rutherford, and Oakville. Guests enjoy multi-course meals prepared onboard by award-winning chefs while taking in panoramic views of the valley through vintage Pullman railcars and the elevated Vista Dome car.
attractions.hours: Station Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Daily). Lunch experiences depart at 11:30 AM (3 hours). Dinner experiences depart at 6:20 PM (2.5 hours). The Legacy Experience is a 6-hour daily excursion.
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by experience and season: Gourmet Express Lunch/Dinner: approximately $225–$295 per person; Vista Dome Lunch/Dinner: approximately $350–$400 per person; Legacy Experience: approximately $500–$700+ per person; Children 12 and under on Gourmet Express Lunch: $109. Prices fluctuate based on season and demand. Wine tasting add-ons available for additional cost.
highlights: 14 curated experiences including lunch, dinner, and seasonal offerings
Vista Dome car with 360-degree panoramic views from an elevated observation railcar
Gourmet multi-course meals prepared onboard by Executive Chef Donald Young
Vintage 1952 Pullman railcars restored to early 1900s elegance
Legacy Experience: 6-hour excursion with wine tastings at partner wineries
Seasonal experiences including Santa Train Jolly Journeys and Grape Stomp at Grgich Hills
attractions.tips: Book well in advance, especially for weekend and holiday departures, as experiences sell out quickly. Dress code is smart casual. The Vista Dome car offers the best views but books up fastest. Consider the Legacy Experience for a complete Napa Valley day with winery stops. Lunch departures provide the best natural lighting for photos of the vineyards. Wine is not included in most experiences but can be purchased onboard. Gratuity is not included and is recommended.
attractions.nearby: Oxbow Public Market, Napa downtown shopping district, Napa Valley Museum, Artesa Vineyards & Winery, Domaine Carneros, Castello di Amorosa, Robert Mondavi Winery, Beringer Vineyards, Charles Krug Winery

attractions.name: Old Faithful Geyser of California
attractions.description: Old Faithful Geyser of California is a unique geothermal attraction in Calistoga, Napa Valley, featuring one of only three "Old Faithful" geysers in the world. The geyser erupts approximately every 30-90 minutes, shooting hot water and steam into the air. The park includes a petting zoo, geology museum, picnic areas, and games like bocce ball, making it a family-friendly destination.
attractions.hours: Sunday-Monday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Tuesday-Friday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM
attractions.admission: Adults: $15, Children: $9 (prices based on visitor reviews; check official website for current rates and possible discounts via Groupon)
highlights: One of only three 'Old Faithful' geysers in the world
Geyser erupts naturally every 30-90 minutes with hot water and steam
Petting zoo featuring goats, llamas, alpacas, and horses
Small geology museum with educational exhibits about volcanoes and geysers
Shaded picnic areas with couches, tables, and cabanas
Family games including bocce ball and funhouse mirrors
attractions.tips: Check the eruption schedule upon arrival to plan your visit around a guaranteed geyser display. Bring snacks and drinks as picnicking is encouraged. Check Groupon for discounted tickets. Arrive early to avoid crowds, especially on weekends. The area can get hot in summer, so dress accordingly and use the shaded seating areas while waiting.
attractions.nearby: Calistoga Spa Hot Springs, Bothe-Napa Valley State Park, Sterling Vineyards, Castello di Amorosa, Indian Springs Resort, Calistoga Mud Baths

attractions.name: Petaluma Historic Commercial District
attractions.description: The Petaluma Historic Commercial District is a 17.4-acre National Register of Historic Places-listed historic district in downtown Petaluma, California. Listed in 1995, it encompasses 63 contributing buildings along Petaluma Boulevard between B Street and Prospect Street. The district features Italianate and Classical Revival architecture, including notable structures like the Old Petaluma Opera House, Hotel Petaluma, and the Masonic Building.
attractions.address: Petaluma Boulevard, between B St. and Prospect St.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (outdoor historic district; individual businesses and museums have their own hours)
attractions.admission: Free (public outdoor historic district; some museums and buildings within have their own admission fees)
highlights: 63 contributing historic buildings along Petaluma Boulevard
National Register of Historic Places since 1995
Italianate and Classical Revival architecture
Old Petaluma Opera House (separately listed on NRHP)
Hotel Petaluma - the district's tallest building in Mediterranean Revival style
Masonic Building (1882) with cast iron Italianate detailing
attractions.tips: The district is best explored on foot along Petaluma Boulevard. Free guided walking tours led by costumed docents are available most Saturdays from May through November, meeting at the Petaluma Historical Library & Museum steps at 10:30 AM. Visit the Petaluma Historical Library & Museum (20 Fourth St, open Thu-Sun 10AM-4PM, free/donation) for more context. The area is also home to antique shops, restaurants, and the Petaluma River waterfront. Self-guided walking tours are available online via the museum's History Spots program.
attractions.nearby: Petaluma Historical Library & Museum, Petaluma Adobe State Historic Park, Petaluma River, Olde Petaluma Opera House, Petaluma Creamery Store

attractions.name: Safari West Wildlife Preserve and Tent Camp
attractions.description: Known as "The Sonoma Serengeti," Safari West brings the magic of Africa to California's Wine Country, offering guests the thrill of a safari without leaving the country. Visitors ride aboard open-air safari vehicles through a 400-acre preserve home to over 900 exotic animals including giraffes, rhinos, zebras, cheetahs, and more. Now operating as a nonprofit, the preserve also offers African-style glamping tents, on-site dining, and conservation experiences.
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM, 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 1:00 PM, 2:00 PM, 4:00 PM (tour departure times)
attractions.admission: Classic Safari Tour: Adult $120, Senior 62+ $110, Teen 13-17 $45, Child 4-12 $45. Weekday (Mon-Thu) Classic Safari: $110 Adult / $110 Senior / $45 Teen / $45 Child. Children under 4 not permitted on Classic Safari. Group rates (10+) include 10% service charge. Private Safaris available at separate pricing.
highlights: 3-hour guided safari tour through 400-acre preserve with over 900 exotic animals
Home to giraffes, white rhinos, cheetahs, zebras, Cape buffalo, lemurs, and more
African-style glamping tent camp with 30 private tents imported from Botswana
Walk-through aviary with international bird species and primate exhibits
On-site South African braai dining at Savannah Café
USA Today 10 Best Glamping Spots #1 winner
attractions.tips: Advanced reservations required — book well in advance as tours frequently sell out. Children must be at least 4 years old (45 inches tall for top seat). Bring sunscreen, hat, and layers as weather can change. Wi-Fi and cell service are limited (AT&T works best). Service animals allowed but must be arranged 72 hours in advance. Pets not permitted. Tours are unscripted and vary each visit.
attractions.nearby: Sonoma County wineries, Healdsburg, Santa Rosa's Railroad Square, Charles M. Schulz Museum, California Welcome Center Sonoma County, Korbel Champagne Cellars, Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve, Jack London State Historic Park

attractions.name: Sonoma State Historic Park
attractions.description: Sonoma State Historic Park is a California State Park located in the center of Sonoma, California, in the heart of wine country. The park consists of six historic sites: Mission San Francisco Solano, Sonoma Barracks (El Cuartel de Sonoma), Blue Wing Inn, La Casa Grande, Lachryma Montis (Vallejo Estate), and the Toscano Hotel. Founded in 1909, the park preserves the legacy of Mariano Guadalupe Vallejo and the birthplace of the California Bear Flag Revolt.
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week; Closed Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day
attractions.admission: Adult Museum Entry Fee: $3.00; Youth Museum Entry Fee: $2.00; Children (5 & Under): Free. California State Parks Adventure Pass accepted for free entry.
highlights: Mission San Francisco Solano - the 21st and northernmost California mission, founded in 1823
Sonoma Barracks (El Cuartel de Sonoma) - site of the Bear Flag Revolt in 1846
Blue Wing Inn - one of the first hotels in California north of San Francisco, dating to 1836
Lachryma Montis (Vallejo Estate) - Carpenter Gothic Victorian home of General Mariano Guadalupe Vallejo
La Casa Grande - the Vallejo family's first home in Sonoma
Toscano Hotel - period-furnished hotel complex from the 1870s
attractions.tips: Visit during weekdays for a quieter experience. The park grounds can be explored for free; museum entry fees apply only to enter the historic buildings. Dogs are allowed on trails and in historic areas on leash but not in the visitor center/museum. No alcoholic beverages or drones allowed. Speed limit is 15 mph for all vehicles and bicycles. The California State Parks Adventure Pass provides free entry for 4th graders and their families.
attractions.nearby: Sonoma Plaza, Buena Vista Winery, Sonoma Valley Museum of Art, Cornerstone Sonoma, Jack London State Historic Park, Petaluma Adobe State Historic Park, Bartholomew Park Winery, Ledson Winery & Vineyards

attractions.name: Sonoma TrainTown Railroad
attractions.description: Sonoma TrainTown Railroad is a beloved family-friendly amusement park featuring a quarter-scale miniature railroad on 4 miles of track winding through 10 acres of beautifully landscaped grounds with tunnels, lakes, and evergreen forests. Founded in 1958, the park also offers five amusement rides including a Ferris wheel, carousel, and airplane ride. Located just one mile south of the historic Sonoma Plaza, it has been a staple of the Sonoma community for generations.
attractions.hours: Fall/Winter/Spring (Aug 10, 2025 – June 13, 2026): Saturdays & Sundays, 10am – 4pm. Summer (June 13 – Aug 16, 2026): Daily, 10am – 4pm. Closed when raining.
attractions.days_open: Saturdays and Sundays year-round; daily during summer season (mid-June through mid-August). Closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas, and when raining.
attractions.admission: General admission and parking: FREE. Train Trip: $9.75 per ticket (ages 12 months+). Amusement Rides: $4.25 per ticket or 5 tickets for $18.75. Children under 12 months free on train. All riders require a ticket for amusement rides, including babies and accompanying adults.
highlights: 20-minute miniature train ride through 10 acres of landscaped grounds with tunnels, lakes, and waterfalls
Five amusement rides including Ferris wheel, Iron Horse Carousel, and airplane ride
Petting zoo with hands-on animal encounters
Historic replica structures and scenic gardens
Founded in 1958 and continuously family-owned and operated
Free admission and free parking for all visitors
attractions.tips: Call ahead at 707.938.3912 to confirm the park is open, especially during fall/winter/spring when it closes for rain. No advance reservations are required — tickets are sold on a first-come, first-served basis. Outside food is not permitted, but snacks and meals are available on-site. The train ride is handicap accessible. Plan to arrive early on summer weekends as it can get busy.
attractions.nearby: Sonoma Plaza and historic downtown Sonoma, Charles M. Schulz Museum (Santa Rosa), Sonoma Valley wineries and tasting rooms, Sonoma Mission, Ledson Winery, Benziger Family Winery, Cornerstone Sonoma

attractions.name: The Petrified Forest
attractions.description: The Petrified Forest in Calistoga is one of the finest examples in the world of an ancient forest, featuring majestic redwood trees that were turned to stone approximately 3.4 million years ago by a volcanic eruption from nearby Mount St. Helena. Discovered in 1870, the forest contains some of the largest petrified trees in the world and has been operated by the same family since the early 20th century. Visitors can explore the site via self-guided or docent-led tours along a circular trail through the petrified grove.
attractions.hours: Daily, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (winter) or 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (summer)
attractions.admission: Adult: $14.00 | Senior: $13.00 | Junior: $8.00 | Child: $6.00 | Under 6: Free | Special rates available for groups and families
highlights: Home to some of the largest petrified trees in the world, including 'The Queen' (8 feet wide, 65 feet long) and 'The Monarch' (nearly 300 feet long)
One of the finest preserved ancient forests in the world, as described by the Palynological Society
Self-guided and docent-led tours available on a circular loop trail
On-site museum and nature/gift shop with fossils, crystals, and petrified wood specimens
Created by a volcanic eruption at Mount St. Helena approximately 3.4 million years ago
The only petrified forest in California from the Pliocene epoch
attractions.tips: Visit during cooler morning hours, especially in summer. Combine your trip with nearby Calistoga attractions like Old Faithful Geyser and the hot springs. The gift shop has interesting petrified wood specimens and nature items. Ask about group or family rates if visiting with multiple people. Wear comfortable walking shoes for the trail.
attractions.nearby: Old Faithful Geyser of California, Calistoga hot springs and spas, Mount St. Helena, Bothe-Napa Valley State Park, Castello di Amorosa, Safari West, downtown Calistoga shopping and dining

attractions.name: di Rosa Center for Contemporary Art
attractions.description: di Rosa Center for Contemporary Art is a museum and sculpture park located in the Napa Valley, featuring contemporary art exhibitions, a sculpture meadow, and rotating shows. Founded by Rene and Veronica di Rosa, it preserves and shares their extensive collection of Bay Area figurative and contemporary art. The center also operates a satellite museum, di Rosa SF, in San Francisco's Dogpatch neighborhood.
attractions.hours: Thursday – Sunday: 11 a.m. – 4 p.m. (Last admission 3:30 p.m.)
attractions.admission: $25 General; $20 Seniors, Students, and Military; $5 Minors (Ages 5–17); FREE for Members; FREE for Children Under 5
highlights: 217-acre sculpture meadow with outdoor contemporary art installations
Rotating exhibitions featuring Bay Area figurative and contemporary art
Guided docent tours offered Thursday–Sunday at 11 a.m.
Satellite museum di Rosa SF in San Francisco's Dogpatch neighborhood
Free parking and self-guided access to galleries and grounds
Private tours available by appointment on Tuesdays and Wednesdays
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are encouraged for tours as they can fill up. Last admission is 3:30 p.m., so plan to arrive by early afternoon. Private tours on Tuesdays and Wednesdays require two weeks' advance notice. Parking is free. Ticket refunds are only available if canceled at least 24 hours in advance.
attractions.nearby: Napa Valley wineries, Castello di Amorosa, Calistoga, Oxbow Public Market, Downtown Napa, Carneros wine region, V. Sattui Winery, Schramsberg Vineyards
